note_on_path: cuBLAS GemmEx with mixed FP32+BF16 input types returned status 15 (CUBLAS_STATUS_NOT_SUPPORTED) on cuBLAS 12.4.5 with sm_80. Fallback used: explicit on-device upcast W_bf16->W_f32 then cublasSgemm. This preserves the LayerCast NUMERICAL pattern (BF16 weight storage + FP32 compute) — only the dispatch path is fallback. Future Stage 2 fire may try cuBLAS 12.9+ which adds more mixed-precision algos.
captured_from: bash output capture (script attempted pod re-fire after pod destroy started; pod was destroyed before automated pull). Numbers are direct stdout from r049_layercast_linear binary on the A100 instance — bit-identical to what would have been emitted to JSON.

F-FORGE-RFC049-LAYERCAST-DIVERGE.anchor: LayerCast paper (arxiv 2506.09501) reports <= 3.4% divergence vs full FP32. Our measured worst = 1.5%, well under both.

notes: LayerCast PATTERN test: BF16 weight storage + FP32 compute path (forge mixed-precision substrate Layer 2 + Layer 3 anchor).

On cuBLAS 12.4.5/sm_80, mixed-type GemmEx (FP32 X + BF16 W direct) was NOT_SUPPORTED. Fallback path = explicit on-device upcast bf16->f32 then Sgemm. Numerical pattern preserved — BF16 mantissa truncation is applied at the cast step; downstream matmul is pure FP32. This is exactly the LayerCast 'just-in-time upcast' contract.

Performance: fallback path is 1.24-1.27x SLOWER than pure FP32 reference (extra upcast kernel + memory traffic for W_f32 scratch). On cuBLAS versions with native mixed-type support (12.9+), the direct GemmEx path should be at or faster than reference. This is a Stage 2 follow-up.
Numerical: max mean_rel = 1.51% across 4 shapes; LayerCast paper anchor <= 3.4%. PASS with comfortable margin.
Memory: BF16 weight = 2 B/elem vs FP32 4 B/elem = 0.5x ratio (exact, anchored by sizeof).
